game_name: Might and Magic III: Isles of Terra

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies appear on screen as player approaches from distant squares
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial awareness of threat proximity; allows player to assess and potentially avoid or prepare for combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Player character faces in HUD display different expressions based on character condition
danger_signals.meaning: Party health and status indicators communicated through facial expressions; damage and debuffs visually apparent

danger_signals.visual_form: Turn-based combat visualization with visible enemy positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat state when enemies are engaged

safety_signals.visual_form: Safe locations like Fountain Head (starting town) with NPCs and inn
safety_signals.meaning: Designated safe zones for rest, healing, and quest progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Inns available in towns for character recovery
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to restore health and access services without immediate threat

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present across multiple nacelle worlds; conflict zones throughout Isles of Terra

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy creatures aligned with Sheltem's machinations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nacelles being launched into suns; areas of destruction and devastation
Pyramids of the Ancients; underwater seedship Lincoln; Main Control Center locations
Corak's journals guide player narrative; mysterious guidance throughout quest

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nacelles visible across multiple worlds; destruction and environmental devastation increasing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy creature encounters become more frequent and challenging; multiple world instability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Access to new areas and nacelle worlds unlocked; environmental transitions from surface to underwater locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Encounter difficulty scaling; access to maze sequences and pyramid structures after story progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Restricted save zones with unique visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cannot save game state; increased pressure and commitment required for these encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to submerged ancient technology environments; visual shift from natural to artificial architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Major story revelation moment; access to Main Control Center mechanics

environmental_storytelling.example: Separate flat worlds drawn from the Void demonstrate cosmological story; each represents different threat from Sheltem's schemes
Pyramids of the Ancients communicate advanced civilization heritage and connection to human colonization history
Lincoln vessel represents ancient terraforming technology; discovery location communicates revelation about Terra's origin
Fountain Head starting town establishes civilized hub; contrasts with dangerous wilderness and dungeon environments
Thematic environment name and structure communicate extreme danger; traversal represents ultimate test for 'Ultimate Adventurers' title

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal facial expression in HUD; full combat capability
Altered facial expressions visible in character HUD faces; reduced effectiveness in combat
Visible on screen as player approaches; allows tactical positioning before engagement
Loot automatically collected at combat end; no manual search required; creature permanently removed from level until area reset
Provide quests, services, and narrative guidance; establish safety in civilized zones
Launching nacelles into suns; actively destroying worlds; pilots escape pod at climax

notes: Might and Magic III uses improved first-person isometric perspective with graphical HUD replacing text-based information. Visual communication emphasizes character condition through facial expressions rather than numeric displays, improving immersion. Environmental storytelling dominates narrative delivery through world design rather than dialogue. The game's world-state changes are communicated through accessible areas and visual environmental changes rather than aesthetic transformations of existing spaces. Safety/danger distinction is primarily spatial (towns vs. wilderness) rather than color or symbol-coded.
